codeReview.summary: The new suite exercises important attach/prompt/interrupt/approval/reconnect flows against the real relay HTTP/WS surfaces, a real wrapper binary, and a mock codex process. However, it hard-depends on prebuilt `server/dist` and `wrapper/target/debug` artifacts that are ignored by git and not produced by the documented bot test entrypoint, so the feature is not reproducible from a clean checkout.

issues.description: Lines 18-19 import `startRelayServer` from `../../server/dist/relay-server.js`, and line 32 later launches `wrapper/target/debug/codex-relay-wrapper`. Both are ignored build outputs (`.gitignore:2-3`), but the shared bot test command is only `cd bot && npx vitest run` (`.factory/services.yaml:14`) and init never builds the server package (`.factory/init.sh:53,62`). On a clean checkout or after removing generated files, this suite cannot load the relay server module and therefore does not provide a reproducible integration test path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The shared commands do not encode the new cross-package prebuild dependency introduced by this integration suite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: bot/src/integration-core-flows.test.ts:18-19 imports `../../server/dist/relay-server.js`, but `.factory/services.yaml:14` defines `test-bot` as only `cd bot && npx vitest run`, and `.factory/init.sh:53,62` installs server deps plus builds the wrapper without creating `server/dist`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission metadata now overstates this feature's validation coverage relative to the implemented scenarios.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `features.json:758-781` marks `integration-core-flows` as fulfilling every `VAL-CROSS-001` through `VAL-CROSS-023`, but the added suite contains only three scenarios at `bot/src/integration-core-flows.test.ts:236`, `:351`, and `:484`, so contract items like idle/unattached `/stop`, large-message transport, graceful shutdown, offline attach rejection, and clean session switching are not actually exercised here.

summary: Fail. I reviewed the mission docs, architecture/services guidance, the ts-worker skill, the worker transcript skeleton, and commit 61b8e52a30310d139c97d51a6266201f1927b005. The added integration file covers several core relay flows, but it depends on ignored prebuilt artifacts instead of a reproducible source-level entrypoint, so the feature does not reliably deliver the promised integration test suite from a clean workspace.
